entries.position_summary: Supports enhancing international nuclear security and calls for strengthened global efforts to improve it. Advocates bolstering frameworks and cooperation to reduce nuclear security risks.
entries.quote: Third, efforts should be made to strengthen international nuclear security.

entries.position_summary: Supports eliminating nuclear terrorism through a fair, cooperative global nuclear security system, with states taking primary responsibility by strengthening national capacities and engaging in international cooperation.
entries.quote: Efforts should be made to eliminate the threat of nuclear terrorism and build a fair, cooperative and win-win system of global nuclear security. All countries should earnestly assume primary responsibility in ensuring nuclear security by continuously enhancing their national capacities and actively participating in international cooperation.

entries.position_summary: The country urges stronger safety and security measures for peaceful nuclear facilities, including nuclear power plants. It opposes armed attacks on such facilities, calls for compliance with the Convention on Nuclear Safety and prevention of man-made accidents, and supports a proactive IAEA role in promoting safety and security.
entries.quote: 7. Efforts must be made to ensure the safety and security of nuclear facilities for peaceful uses. Discussions should focus on maintaining the safety and security of nuclear power plants, opposing armed attacks on nuclear power plants and other peaceful nuclear facilities, complying with the Convention on Nuclear Safety, resolutely avoiding man-made nuclear accidents, and supporting the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in playing a positive role in promoting the safety and security of peaceful nuclear facilities.

entries.position_summary: The country calls to protect peaceful nuclear facilities and opposes armed attacks on them. It urges compliance with the Convention on Nuclear Safety, efforts to prevent man-made nuclear accidents, and supports an active IAEA role in enhancing the safety and security of such facilities.
entries.quote: 18. Guarantee the safety of peaceful nuclear facilities and oppose armed attacks against nuclear power plants or other peaceful nuclear facilities. All parties should comply with the Convention on Nuclear Safety (CNS), resolutely avoid man-made nuclear accident, and support IAEA to play a positive role in promoting the safety and security of peaceful nuclear facilities.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes strong national and international efforts on nuclear safety and security, asserting compliance with related obligations and active participation in global mechanisms. It supports converting HEU to LEU for micro‑reactors and assisting countries to minimize HEU use, tailored to their needs and capacities.
entries.quote: China always gives priority to nuclear safety and security and continuously improves its national capabilities in nuclear safety and security with a good track record. At the same time, China faithfully fulfills its international obligations on nuclear safety and security, vigorously promotes international cooperation in nuclear safety and security, and deeply participates in international nuclear safety and security mechanisms and processes. China will continue to participate in cooperation on conversion of HEU to LEU for micro-reactors and support countries in minimizing the use of HEU and reducing nuclear safety and security risks, in light of their own needs, as well as their economic and technological capabilities.

entries.position_summary: Urges all States Parties to join and comply with nuclear safety and security conventions, and encourages those developing advanced and small modular reactors (including portable reactors) to work closely with the IAEA to ensure their safe and secure deployment. Emphasizes responsible, safeguarded expansion of peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: Call on all States Parties to join and abide by conventions and instruments in the fields of nuclear safety and security. State parties developing advanced reactors and small modular reactors including portable reactors are encouraged to enhance cooperation with the IAEA to support the safe and secure deployment of such reactors.

entries.position_summary: The statement expresses concern that AUKUS HEU-fueled submarine cooperation could set a precedent for how Paragraph 14 is applied and may entail HEU transfers outside safeguards, calling for comprehensive, inclusive, Member State–led discussions within the IAEA on any safeguards arrangements. It questions whether the IAEA Secretariat can interpret Paragraph 14 without Member States and warns that HEU naval fuel could undermine global HEU minimization efforts emphasized at ICONS 2024.
entries.quote: 4. Regarding the implications of AUKUS nuclear-powered submarine cooperation, some views stated that given the unprecedented nature of the AUKUS collaboration involving transfer of large quantities of weapons-grade highly-enriched uranium outside of safeguards; any safeguards approach to be developed would define future application of Paragraph 14 on nuclear-powered submarine acquisition programmes. Hence, professional technical and governmental open-ended discussions among Member States within the Agency framework are deemed necessary and essential. Some views expressed concerns regarding the implications of HEU fuelled nuclear-powered submarine programmes may have on the future implementation of policies about the minimization of the use of HEU in nuclear applications on which wide ministerial level support is expected at the forthcoming International Conference on Nuclear Security (ICONS2024) organized by the IAEA in Vienna. *** The divergence of the aforementioned viewpoints highlights the importance and necessity of a comprehensive, inclusive and transparent intergovernmental discussion process on AUKUS. The discussion on the AUKUS case-study and its proposed “arrangement” on the non-application of safeguards is only the beginning of such process. The following questions were raised during the workshop, these warrant further in-depth thinking and discussion involving of all interested IAEA Member States:  Who has the right or authority to interpret Paragraph 14 of INFCIRC/153 (Corr.)? Does the IAEA Secretariat have the authority or mandate to interpret the provisions of the NPT without the involvement of Member States?  How would “a non-proscribed military activity” be defined, and by whom?  What is the definition of “the Agency”: is it the Member States, the Director General, the Secretariat; or is the Agency the collectivity of the Member States along with the Director 5/7

entries.position_summary: The country links safe and secure nuclear operations to sustainable peaceful uses, calling for a fair, cooperative international nuclear security system. It urges all states to fulfill national responsibilities for nuclear safety and adopt a balanced, coordinated approach to nuclear security to protect human health and the environment.
entries.quote: Nuclear safety and security are the lifeline for sustainable peaceful uses of nuclear energy, thus the international community should work together to adopt a rational, coordinated and balanced approach to nuclear security, jointly create an international nuclear security system that is fair, cooperative, and mutually beneficial, and build a community with a shared future for nuclear security. All countries should strictly fulfil their national responsibilities for nuclear safety, so that peaceful uses of nuclear energy contributes to creating a community of life for human and nature, rather than sacrifice the natural environment and human health.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes protecting NNWS rights to peaceful nuclear energy, supports the IAEA’s central role, and backs efforts to ensure safety and security of peaceful facilities. It opposes unilateral export restrictions and broader decoupling, and condemns armed attacks on peaceful nuclear facilities.
entries.quote: Fourthly, remain faithful to NPT's initial mission, contribute to peace and development. The rights of peaceful uses of nuclear energy of non-nuclear-weapon States should be effectively protected. As a major country in nuclear science and technology, China supports the “Atoms for Global South Initiative" and supports the central role of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA). We resolutely oppose any misuse of unilateral export restrictions that impedes nuclear energy global collaborative efforts. Nuclear technology should serve as wealth for shared and win-win benefits rather than a tool to weave an iron curtain. Decoupling will ultimately lead to self-isolation. China opposes armed attacks against peaceful nuclear facilities, supports the IAEA in playing a constructive role in promoting the safety and security of peaceful nuclear facilities.

entries.position_summary: China emphasizes protecting NNWS rights to peaceful nuclear energy, supports the IAEA’s central role and the Atoms for Global South initiative, and opposes unilateral export restrictions and decoupling that hinder cooperation. It also opposes armed attacks on peaceful nuclear facilities and backs the IAEA’s role in ensuring their safety and security.
entries.quote: Fourthly, remain faithful to NPT's initial mission, contribute to peace and development. The rights of peaceful uses of nuclear energy of non-nuclear-weapon States should be effectively protected. As a major country in nuclear science and technology, China supports the “Atoms for Global South Initiative" and supports the central role of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA). We resolutely oppose any misuse of unilateral export restrictions that impedes nuclear energy global collaborative efforts. Nuclear technology should serve as wealth for shared and win-win benefits rather than a tool to weave an iron curtain. Decoupling will ultimately lead to self-isolation. China opposes armed attacks against peaceful nuclear facilities, supports the IAEA in playing a constructive role in promoting the safety and security of peaceful nuclear facilities.

entries.position_summary: Supports voluntary minimization of civilian HEU use and stockpiles where feasible, indicating a preference for converting to lower-enriched alternatives to reduce proliferation and security risks while maintaining peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: Encourage relevant State Parties, where technically and economically feasible, to voluntarily minimize the civilian use and stockpiling of highly enriched uranium.

entries.position_summary: The country supports the IAEA taking a proactive role to advance nuclear safety and nuclear security.
entries.quote: Support the IEAE in playing a proactive role in advancing nuclear safety and security.
